title: श्री रक्षाबन्धन स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रक्षाबन्धन स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ रक्षासूत्र स्तवनम् ॥

येन बद्धो बली राजा दानवेन्द्रो महाबलः।
तेन त्वामनुबध्नामि रक्षे मा चल मा चल॥ १॥

श्रावणस्य पौर्णमास्यां रक्षाबन्धनमुत्तमम्।
भगिनीभ्रातृसम्बन्धं शाश्वतं धर्मबन्धनम्॥ २॥

रक्षासूत्रं प्रबध्नामि सुभद्रा यदवीशिने।
बन्धयित्वा यथा भ्रातुः आयुः कीर्तिर्विवर्धते॥ ३॥

॥ लक्ष्मी रक्षा स्तुतिः ॥

लक्ष्मीर्या लोकपालानां धनदस्य गृहे स्थिता।
रक्षासूत्रप्रदात्री सा मम रक्षां करोतु मे॥ ४॥

सा पद्मवासिनी देवी रक्षाबन्धनदेवता।
भगिनीनां स्नेहरूपा भ्रातॄणां बलदायिनी॥ ५॥

सौभाग्यदा शुभा लक्ष्मीः सर्वमंगलकारिणी।
रक्षाबन्धनपर्वणि प्रसीदतु ममोपरि॥ ६॥

॥ भगिनी-भ्रातृ प्रार्थना ॥

भगिनी भ्रातरं रक्षेत् भ्राता रक्षेत् सहोदरीम्।
परस्परं स्नेहबद्धौ चिरंजीवौ सुखी सदा॥ ७॥

आयुष्मान् भव भो भ्रातः कीर्तिमान् धनवान् भव।
रक्षासूत्रप्रभावेन शत्रुभ्यो विजयं लभ॥ ८॥

भ्रातस्ते मंगलं भूयात् सौख्यं सर्वार्थसिद्धिदम्।
दीर्घायुश्च यशश्चैव रक्षाबन्धनभावतः॥ ९॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥

श्रावणे पौर्णमास्यां तु पठेद्यः स्तोत्रमुत्तमम्।
भ्रातृभगिनीसौहार्दं वर्धते नित्यमेव हि॥

रक्षाबन्धनमाहात्म्यात् सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्यते॥

॥ इति श्री रक्षाबन्धन स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Raksha Bandhan Stotram ||

|| Raksha-Sutra Stavanam ||

Yena baddho Bali raja danavendro mahabalah |
Tena tvam anubadhnami rakshe ma chala ma chala || 1 ||

Shravanasya paurnimasyam raksha-bandhanam uttamam |
Bhagini-bhratri-sambandham shashvatam dharma-bandhanam || 2 ||

Raksha-sutram prabadhnami Subhadra yad-avishane |
Bandhayitva yatha bhratuh ayuh kirtir vivardhate || 3 ||

|| Lakshmi Raksha Stutih ||

Lakshmeer ya loka-palanam Dhanadasya grihe sthita |
Raksha-sutra-pradatri sa mama raksham karotu me || 4 ||

Sa Padma-vasini Devi Raksha-bandhana-devata |
Bhagininam sneha-rupa bhratrinam bala-dayini || 5 ||

Saubhagyada shubha Lakshmih sarva-mangala-karini |
Raksha-bandhana-parvani prasidatu mamopari || 6 ||

|| Bhagini-Bhratri Prarthana ||

Bhagini bhrataram rakshet bhrata rakshet sahodarim |
Parasparam sneha-baddhau chiranjeevau sukhi sada || 7 ||

Ayushman bhava bho bhratah kirtiman dhanavan bhava |
Raksha-sutra-prabhavena shatrubhyo vijayam labha || 8 ||

Bhratas te mangalam bhuyat saukhyam sarvartha-siddhidam |
Dirghayush cha yashash chaiva Raksha-bandhana-bhavatah || 9 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||

Shravane paurnimasyam tu pathed yah stotram uttamam |
Bhratri-bhagini-sauhaardam vardhate nityam eva hi |

Raksha-bandhana-mahatmyat sarvapadbhyo vimuchyate ||

|| Iti Shri Raksha Bandhan Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: रक्षाबन्धन पर्व पर पठनीय इस स्तोत्र में तीन भाग हैं। प्रथम भाग में रक्षासूत्र का महत्व बताया गया है - जैसे इन्द्राणी ने राजा बलि को और सुभद्रा ने कृष्ण को राखी बाँधी, वैसे ही बहन भाई को रक्षासूत्र बाँधती है। द्वितीय भाग में लक्ष्मी देवी की स्तुति है जो रक्षाबन्धन की अधिष्ठात्री देवी हैं, बहनों के स्नेह की स्वरूपा और भाइयों को बल देने वाली हैं। तृतीय भाग में बहन-भाई की परस्पर रक्षा की प्रार्थना है - भाई को दीर्घायु, कीर्ति, धन और शत्रुओं पर विजय की कामना की गई है। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से भ्रातृ-भगिनी का सौहार्द बढ़ता है और सब विपत्तियों से मुक्ति मिलती है।
benefits: Strengthens the sacred bond between brother and sister
Invokes Lakshmi's blessings for family prosperity
Invokes longevity, fame and victory to the brother
Traditionally invoked for protection from all calamities and enemies
Invokes the blessing of auspiciousness on the raksha bandhan occasion
Promotes lifelong love and mutual protection in siblings
description: Raksha Bandhan Stotram (श्री रक्षाबन्धन स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi. Attributed to Traditional Festival Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke Lakshmi's blessings for family prosperity as well as protect from all calamities and enemies. Additionally, it is known to strengthen the sacred bond between brother and sister. This sacred text is ideally recited during Raksha Bandhan for enhanced blessings. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Raksha Bandhan Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Raksha Bandhan Stotram in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for the Rakhi festival invoking Lakshmi's blessings.

seoFaqs.question: What is the traditional Raksha Bandhan mantra and what does it mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Sanskrit mantra recited while tying the rakhi is: 'Yena baddho Bali raja danavendro mahabalah, tena tvam pratibadhnami raksha ma chala ma chala' which translates as 'With the same sacred thread that bound the mighty King Bali, the lord of demons, I now tie you. May this protection never falter, never falter.' The mantra invokes the foundational story in which Goddess Lakshmi tied a protective thread on King Bali's wrist. By repeating the same act with the same mantra, the sister extends to her brother the same divine protection that secured Bali, asking that the protective bond hold steady through all circumstances.

seoFaqs.question: What is the puranic story behind the Raksha Bandhan mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The story is told in the Bhavishya Purana and other sources. After Vishnu in his Vamana avatar pushed King Bali to the netherworld, Bali requested that Vishnu remain with him as a guardian. Lakshmi, missing her Lord, devised a strategy: she went to Bali's palace disguised as a distressed woman, was welcomed as a sister, and on the auspicious Shravana Purnima tied a protective thread (raksha sutra) on Bali's wrist. As dakshina she requested the release of her husband. Bali, bound by the sacred bond of brother-sister duty he had just accepted, gladly released Vishnu. The raksha thus became established as the eternal symbol of mutual protection between brother and sister.

seoFaqs.question: When and how is the Raksha Bandhan ritual performed at home?
seoFaqs.answer: The ritual takes place on Shravana Purnima (the full moon of the month of Shravan, usually August). The traditional procedure: prepare a thali with the rakhi, akshat (unbroken rice), kumkum, a small lamp, and sweets; the brother sits facing east; the sister applies tilak with kumkum and akshat to his forehead, performs aarti, ties the rakhi on his right wrist while reciting the raksha mantra, and offers him sweets. The brother in turn gives a small gift and pledges protection. The mantra is the doctrinal heart of the act, transforming a familial gesture into a Vedic protective rite.

seoFaqs.question: Is the raksha-thread tradition limited to brothers and sisters?
seoFaqs.answer: Originally the raksha sutra tradition was much broader than the modern brother-sister focus. Vedic sources describe priests tying protective threads on patrons during yagnas; gurus on disciples; mothers on children; rulers' priests on rulers before battle. The Bhavishya Purana story of Lakshmi and Bali is itself an example of a non-blood relationship sealed by raksha. In contemporary practice the brother-sister application has become predominant, but many traditions continue the broader use: priests tying raksha on devotees during the Shravana Purnima Upakarma in Vedic communities, and gurus tying raksha on disciples. The mantra works in all these contexts.

seoFaqs.question: What protections does the Raksha Bandhan stotram traditionally invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: Beyond the symbolic familial bond, the raksha mantra invokes a comprehensive Vedic protection: from accidents and disease, from enemies and ill-wishers, from planetary afflictions, from natural calamities, and from the influences of malevolent forces. The mantra explicitly compares the rakhi-thread to the bond that held Bali, the most powerful king of his age, indicating that the protection is meant to be similarly unbreakable. The closing words 'raksha ma chala ma chala' (do not falter, do not falter) make it a continuing pledge rather than a one-day ritual: the protection is asked to remain in force throughout the year until the next Raksha Bandhan.
